summary: 2026-07-16 01:15:02 PM PDT — One blocker found: malformed or oversized JSON bypasses both signature verification and the new rate limiter because global body parsing runs first. The HMAC implementation, replay window, raw-byte use for parsed JSON, and URL-validation response otherwise match the current acceptance criteria. The test suite could not execute in this read-only sandbox because listening sockets and temporary-file writes are denied.

findings.title: Body-parser failures bypass the webhook rate limiter
findings.claim: The global `express.json()` middleware runs before the deauthorization router mounted at line 134. Consequently, malformed JSON is rejected with 400 and oversized JSON with 413 before the route-level limiter in `deauth.js` runs. Repeated requests of either kind never increment the limiter and never reach 429, leaving a public pre-auth parsing path outside AC10's bounded ceiling. A malformed request with a missing or invalid signature also bypasses AC1's required 401 signature path.
findings.suggestion: Move this webhook ahead of the global JSON parser and give it a route-local chain ordered as rate limiter, bounded raw-body capture/parser, signature verification, then event dispatch. Add tests showing malformed and oversized JSON requests are counted and eventually return 429; also define the post-limit malformed-body response consistently with AC1.
